threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch at the sound of the 106 bus screeching to a halt, thinking it’s an emergency. Locals don’t even blink.
The 133 driver last night was singing along to the radio like it was a concert. Passengers loved it, but I swear we missed our stop because of his solo.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I’m really in Neukölln was when I saw the kebab shop owner and the barista at the corner café arguing over whose late-night menu was better.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a Döner at 4 AM post-clubbing and watch street artists turn trash into gallery-worthy pieces on the sidewalk.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can always spot a true local—they’ll dodge the tourists at Dolores Park like pros, while the newcomers stand in the middle, lost with maps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the N-Judah coaching a lost tourist on the best burrito spots. Only in SF do strangers bond over tacos on public transport.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think the plaça is just for selfies, but locals use it to catch up, sip vermut, and watch the world go by. Totally different vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by how they still treat the metro like it's a sightseeing tour, staring at maps instead of just following the crowd.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep waiting for the bus on Kingsland Road like it’s going to arrive on time. Local folks know it’s a gamble at best.
Caught a late night 333 last week; a lad dropped his burger on the floor and insisted it was still fine to eat. The bus driver just shook his head.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the kebab shop owner and the café barista share a smoke break, laughing in the middle of a workday. That's Neukölln in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find snack stands selling currywurst and vegan döner side by side, catering to the fiercest food rivalries with zero shame.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate BART delays; they’ll just pull out a book instead of panicking like tourists.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni held up by a dog on the tracks yesterday—classic SF. Everyone laughed, but you could see the frustration in some faces.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'Gràcia' is just the squares and bars; they miss the tiny art studios tucked into the side streets where locals create magic.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitation at the 'tapa' ordering ritual—staring at the menu like it's a riddle instead of just diving in.
